arts.ttl: Daniel Maldini’s rise to the national team: How a summer decision changed everything
arts.sum: Daniel Maldini’s call-up to the Italian national team marks a significant milestone in his career and continues a remarkable family legacy. The young midfielder, now playing for Monza, has earned his first call-up from coach Luciano Spalletti for the upcoming Nations League matches against Belgium and Israel. Calciomercato report how Alessandro Nesta, a football legend himself, speaks highly of Maldini, describing him as a “special player, different from others.” Nesta credits Maldini’s improved form to the consistent playing time he’s receiving at Monza, which has allowed him to find the continuity he previously lacked. arts.sum: Under-pressure Roma coach Ivan Juric meets Alessandro Nesta’s Monza, the only Serie A team without...

Roma have won three of their last four games (D1) against Monza in Serie A; the Giallorossi could become the first team that the Brianza side have faced five times in the tournament without ever winning. Meanwhile, the Giallorossi have won both of their league matches – against Udinese and Venezia – since the appointment of Ivan Juric and are aiming for a third consecutive victory to go into their big clash against Inter after the international break with real momentum.

arts.ttl: Roma Set to Take on Monza Without Attacking Mainstone
arts.sum: Roma will seek their third consecutive Serie A triumph under new manager Ivan Juric without standout performer Paulo Dybala. The Giallorossi took to X to announce their matchday squad for Sunday's fixture against Monza, with the Argentine's name left out. The post Roma Set to Take on Monza Without Attacking Mainstone appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.

arts.ttl: Roma face setback as Dybala and Hummels ruled out for Monza match
arts.sum: Roma have been dealt a blow ahead of their Serie A match against Monza, with key players Paulo Dybala and Mats Hummels both absent from the squad list. Dybala, who missed Roma’s previous league fixture against Venezia, was expected to make his return but has been sidelined once again. The Argentine forward experienced discomfort during training, prompting the club to take a cautious approach with the international break looming. Roma’s medical staff decided not to risk aggravating Dybala’s condition, opting to give him more time to recover.
